responseSchema.description: Business calls settings for a location.

callsState.description: Required. The state of this location's enrollment in Business calls.

consentTime.description: Input only. Time when the end user provided consent to the API user to enable business calls.

name.description: Required. The resource name of the calls settings. Format: locations/{location}/businesscallssettings

businessCallsInsights.description: A collection of business calls insights for the location.

items.description: Insights for calls made to a location.

aggregateMetrics.description: Metrics aggregated over the input time range.

endDate.description: Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following: * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values. * A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary). * A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. * A year and month, with a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: * google.type.TimeOfDay * google.type.DateTime * google.protobuf.Timestamp

day.description: Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0 to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't significant.

month.description: Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a month and day.

year.description: Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without a year.

hourlyMetrics.description: A list of metrics by hour of day.

hour.description: Hour of the day. Allowed values are 0-23.

missedCallsCount.description: Total count of missed calls for this hour.

startDate.description: Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following: * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values. * A month and day, with a zero year (for example, an anniversary). * A year on its own, with a zero month and a zero day. * A year and month, with a zero day (for example, a credit card expiration date). Related types: * google.type.TimeOfDay * google.type.DateTime * google.protobuf.Timestamp

day.description: Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0 to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't significant.

month.description: Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a month and day.

year.description: Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without a year.

weekdayMetrics.description: A list of metrics by day of week.

day.description: Day of the week. Allowed values are Sunday - Saturday.

missedCallsCount.description: Total count of missed calls for this hour.

metricType.description: The metric for which the value applies.

name.description: Required. The resource name of the calls insights. Format: locations/{location}/businesscallsinsights

nextPageToken.description: A token, which can be sent as `page_token` to retrieve the next page. If this field is omitted, there are no subsequent pages. Some of the metric_types (e.g, AGGREGATE_COUNT) returns a single page. For these metrics, the next_page_token will be empty.

consentTime.description: Input only. Time when the end user provided consent to the API user to enable business calls.

callsState.description: Required. The state of this location's enrollment in Business calls.
